body.text: A variable is a named container for a value. The program can read it, change it, and use it later. Without variables, every action starts from zero — the program has no memory.

body.desc: Create a variable named 'count' and store the value 0 inside it
Take what is in 'count', add 1, and store the result back

body.label: Key rule
body.text: Reassigning a variable replaces the old value entirely. It does not add a second value alongside the first.

exercises.title: Predict the value
tasks: On paper first — no editor yet. Work through this line by line: let score = 10 → score = score + 5 → score = score * 2 → score = score - 4
Write down the value of score after each line.
Now open the editor. Type each line and use console.log(score) after each one to check your prediction.
Where did your prediction go wrong, if anywhere? Discuss.
exercises.hint: Work left to right. After line 1 score is 10. After line 2 it is 15. Keep going.

checks.name: score
checks.label: score ends up as 26 after all four operations

exercises.title: Write three variables
tasks: Open a fresh editor. Write three variables: your name, your age, and your favorite number.
Write a fourth variable called message that joins all three into a sentence.
Now change your age variable to a different number and run it again.
exercises.hint: Join strings and numbers with +. Example: 'I am ' + age + ' years old.'

checks.label: something is logged to the console
